mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a single quarter, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings revisions downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp rise in consumer credit balances indicate a supply-demand mismatch where inventory drawdowns outpace spending, compressing corporate margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of declining housing starts on financial conditions, accelerating equity valuation compression and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a week.
failure_condition: The mechanism produces positive forward returns in >55% of triggered episodes, or the cross-sectional correlation between rate hikes and housing declines shows no amplification effect on equity volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP expands aggregate corporate revenue bases, directly supporting earnings per share growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no positive correlation with subsequent 60-day equity returns, or the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for three consecutive months while the unemployment rate remains below 5.0%, it signals an overheating labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes, ultimately triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over a month.
failure_condition: The dual-threshold condition fails to precede negative equity returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or forward returns under this regime are statistically indistinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment where CPI growth falls below 2.0% annually, a sustained rise in industrial production indicates productive capacity expansion rather than demand-pull inflation, which compresses input costs and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a week.
failure_condition: Rising industrial output during low inflation periods fails to generate positive equity returns, or the cost-compression channel shows no measurable impact on sectoral profit margins.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in total nonfarm employment reflects expanding business activity and wage growth, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Sequential employment gains show no positive association with subsequent 20-day equity returns, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes.
